title: Tractor Supply (TSCO) Misses Q1 Earnings and Revenue Estimates

title: Tractor Supply stock extends pullback after profit and sales miss expectations, due to ‘less favorable' spring weather
text: Shares of Tractor Supply Co. TSCO dropped 6.3% in premarket trading, putting them on track to suffer a third-straight loss after closing Monday at a record high, after the rural-lifestyle retailer missed profit and sales expectations, as “less favorable” spring weather trends delayed the spring selling season. Net income for the quarter to April 1 came in at $183.1 million, or $1.65 a share, versus $187.2 million, or $1.65 a share, in the year-ago period.
